evals.name: high-activity-multi-tool-project
evals.prompt:
I just ran /code-insights extract recent --hours 72. The database shows project 7988f69c3463b2d9 with 104 sessions across 4 different AI tools (crush, gemini-cli, mistral-vibe, opencode). The dominant workflow pattern is "explore-then-build" with 25 sessions, followed by "direct-execution" (25 sessions) and "iterative-refinement" (19 sessions).

Create an Obsidian dashboard that shows:
- Timeline of sessions grouped by source tool
- Workflow pattern distribution
- Transition points between tools (when did the user switch?)
- A canvas visualization showing how the workflows evolved

Also, generate a friction analysis - I see repeated-mistakes patterns appearing multiple times.
evals.expected_output: Dashboard at ~/Notebook/Dashboards/7988f69c-Sessions.md with timeline, workflow distribution, and tool transitions. Canvas file showing workflow evolution. Friction analysis with actionable insights.

evals.name: knowledge-gap-friction-pattern
evals.prompt:
/code-insights extract found 17 sessions for project ceda22e2013e495c over the last 5 days. The friction analysis shows:
- 3 instances of "knowledge-gap" (misreading git status, schema validation)
- 2 instances of "stale-assumptions" (wrong working directory)
- 1 "repeated-mistakes" (premature resolution claims)

The sessions involved claude-code, gemini-cli, mistral-vibe, and crush.

Synthesize this into a dashboard focusing on:
1. What knowledge gaps emerged and how they were resolved
2. Which tool handled each friction type best
3. Actionable learnings to prevent these gaps in future sessions
4. Extract any prompt_quality insights (avg confidence 0.85+)

Then create a NotebookLM import of these sessions so I can ask questions about why these patterns keep recurring.
evals.expected_output: Dashboard with friction-focused analysis. NotebookLM sources (17 session exports). Action items extracted from insights with confidence >= 0.85.

evals.name: multi-project-insight-correlation
evals.prompt:
I extracted insights from code-insights across 3 projects:
1. 7988f69c3463b2d9: 554 insights (learning, decision, summary, prompt_quality)
2. 1519a1278debf7e3: 134 insights
3. e3b0c44298fc1c14: 128 insights

All three projects show similar insight types but I want to understand the patterns. Create dashboards for each project, then generate a CROSS-PROJECT canvas showing:
- Common learnings across all three
- Decision patterns that differ between projects
- Prompt quality improvements over time
- Which workflow patterns correlate with high-quality insights

Focus on insights with confidence >= 0.9 for the cross-project analysis.
evals.expected_output: 3 project dashboards (one per project). 1 cross-project canvas with insight correlation. Summary of common vs. unique patterns.
